+            # Adding multiple creators if available
+            if self.workflow.creator_metadata:
+                for creator_data in self.workflow.creator_metadata:
+                    if creator_data.get("class") == "Person":
+                        # Create the person entity
+                        creator_entity = crate.add(
+                            ContextEntity(
+                                crate,
+                                creator_data.get("identifier", ""),  # Default to empty string if identifier is missing
+                                properties={
+                                    "@type": "Person",
+                                    "name": creator_data.get("name", ""),  # Default to empty string if name is missing
+                                    "orcid": creator_data.get(
+                                        "identifier", ""
+                                    ),  # Assuming identifier is ORCID, or adjust as needed
+                                    "url": creator_data.get("url", ""),  # Add URL if available, otherwise empty string
+                                    "email": creator_data.get(
+                                        "email", ""
+                                    ),  # Add email if available, otherwise empty string
+                                },
+                            )
+                        )
+                        # Append the person creator entity to the mainEntity
+                        crate.mainEntity.append_to("creator", creator_entity)
+
+                    elif creator_data.get("class") == "Organization":
+                        # Create the organization entity
+                        organization_entity = crate.add(
+                            ContextEntity(
+                                crate,
+                                creator_data.get(
+                                    "url", ""
+                                ),  # Use URL as identifier if available, otherwise empty string
+                                properties={
+                                    "@type": "Organization",
+                                    "name": creator_data.get("name", ""),  # Default to empty string if name is missing
+                                    "url": creator_data.get("url", ""),  # Add URL if available, otherwise empty string
+                                },
+                            )
+                        )
+                        # Append the organization entity to the mainEntity
+                        crate.mainEntity.append_to("creator", organization_entity)
+
+            # Add CWL workflow entity if exists
+            crate.mainEntity["subjectOf"] = cwl_wf
+
+        # Add tools used in the workflow
+        self._add_tools(crate)

we go again !
I am launching this PR on a bit of work I did to ameliorate the RO-Crate plugin.

I updated the `_add_worfklow` function. I added the information on the author of the workflow:
- Name
- Orcid
The update checks if the workflow attribute 'creator_metadata' exist. If it exists, it writes into the ro-crate the name of the creator (if there is no name it leaves a "") same for the idenifier (by default i put the ORCID maybe it's not a good Idea and if there is no Orcid it leaves a ""). If there is no  'creator_metadata' nothing is written on the creator.

I created a function `_add_tools` that checks the different info in `workflow.steps` to add some tools details:
- Id of the tool
- tool's name (label if there is one or id if there is no label)
- tools' version
- tool's description: the update checks if the tool has an annotations attribute if it exist it is written as the description if it does not the description remains "")
- url to the the toolshed (not sure this part is relevant as I did not yet solve how to have the exact url to the tool in the toolshed and I just let the global url to the toolshed)

I am not sure if I wrote that the correct way corresponding to the run crate profile (this is a bit obscure to me)

@pauldg What do you think of this ?
Thanks !!

we go again !
I am launching this PR on a bit of work I did to ameliorate the RO-Crate plugin.

I updated the `_add_worfklow` function. I added the information on the author of the workflow:
- Name
- Orcid
The update checks if the workflow attribute 'creator_metadata' exist. If it exists, it writes into the ro-crate the name of the creator (if there is no name it leaves a "") same for the idenifier (by default i put the ORCID maybe it's not a good Idea and if there is no Orcid it leaves a ""). If there is no  'creator_metadata' nothing is written on the creator.

I created a function `_add_tools` that checks the different info in `workflow.steps` to add some tools details:
- Id of the tool
- tool's name (label if there is one or id if there is no label)
- tools' version
- tool's description: the update checks if the tool has an annotations attribute if it exist it is written as the description if it does not the description remains "")
- url to the the toolshed (not sure this part is relevant as I did not yet solve how to have the exact url to the tool in the toolshed and I just let the global url to the toolshed)

I am not sure if I wrote that the correct way corresponding to the run crate profile (this is a bit obscure to me)

@pauldg What do you think of this ?
Thanks !!

we go again !
I am launching this PR on a bit of work I did to ameliorate the RO-Crate plugin.

I updated the `_add_worfklow` function. I added the information on the author of the workflow:
- Name
- Orcid
The update checks if the workflow attribute 'creator_metadata' exist. If it exists, it writes into the ro-crate the name of the creator (if there is no name it leaves a "") same for the idenifier (by default i put the ORCID maybe it's not a good Idea and if there is no Orcid it leaves a ""). If there is no  'creator_metadata' nothing is written on the creator.

I created a function `_add_tools` that checks the different info in `workflow.steps` to add some tools details:
- Id of the tool
- tool's name (label if there is one or id if there is no label)
- tools' version
- tool's description: the update checks if the tool has an annotations attribute if it exist it is written as the description if it does not the description remains "")
- url to the the toolshed (not sure this part is relevant as I did not yet solve how to have the exact url to the tool in the toolshed and I just let the global url to the toolshed)

I am not sure if I wrote that the correct way corresponding to the run crate profile (this is a bit obscure to me)

@pauldg What do you think of this ?
Thanks !!

we go again !
I am launching this PR on a bit of work I did to ameliorate the RO-Crate plugin.

I updated the `_add_worfklow` function. I added the information on the author of the workflow:
- Name
- Orcid
The update checks if the workflow attribute 'creator_metadata' exist. If it exists, it writes into the ro-crate the name of the creator (if there is no name it leaves a "") same for the idenifier (by default i put the ORCID maybe it's not a good Idea and if there is no Orcid it leaves a ""). If there is no  'creator_metadata' nothing is written on the creator.

I created a function `_add_tools` that checks the different info in `workflow.steps` to add some tools details:
- Id of the tool
- tool's name (label if there is one or id if there is no label)
- tools' version
- tool's description: the update checks if the tool has an annotations attribute if it exist it is written as the description if it does not the description remains "")
- url to the the toolshed (not sure this part is relevant as I did not yet solve how to have the exact url to the tool in the toolshed and I just let the global url to the toolshed)

I am not sure if I wrote that the correct way corresponding to the run crate profile (this is a bit obscure to me)

@pauldg What do you think of this ?
Thanks !!

I am launching this PR on a bit of work I did to ameliorate the RO-Crate plugin.

I updated the `_add_worfklow` function. I added the information on the author of the workflow:
- Name
- Orcid
The update checks if the workflow attribute 'creator_metadata' exist. If it exists, it writes into the ro-crate the name of the creator (if there is no name it leaves a "") same for the idenifier (by default i put the ORCID maybe it's not a good Idea and if there is no Orcid it leaves a ""). If there is no  'creator_metadata' nothing is written on the creator.

I created a function `_add_tools` that checks the different info in `workflow.steps` to add some tools details:
- Id of the tool
- tool's name (label if there is one or id if there is no label)
- tools' version
- tool's description: the update checks if the tool has an annotations attribute if it exist it is written as the description if it does not the description remains "")
- url to the the toolshed (not sure this part is relevant as I did not yet solve how to have the exact url to the tool in the toolshed and I just let the global url to the toolshed)

I am not sure if I wrote that the correct way corresponding to the run crate profile (this is a bit obscure to me)

@pauldg What do you think of this ?
Thanks !!

we go again !
I am launching this PR on a bit of work I did to ameliorate the RO-Crate plugin.

I updated the `_add_worfklow` function. I added the information on the author of the workflow:
- Name
- Orcid
The update checks if the workflow attribute 'creator_metadata' exist. If it exists, it writes into the ro-crate the name of the creator (if there is no name it leaves a "") same for the idenifier (by default i put the ORCID maybe it's not a good Idea and if there is no Orcid it leaves a ""). If there is no  'creator_metadata' nothing is written on the creator.

I created a function `_add_tools` that checks the different info in `workflow.steps` to add some tools details:
- Id of the tool
- tool's name (label if there is one or id if there is no label)
- tools' version
- tool's description: the update checks if the tool has an annotations attribute if it exist it is written as the description if it does not the description remains "")
- url to the the toolshed (not sure this part is relevant as I did not yet solve how to have the exact url to the tool in the toolshed and I just let the global url to the toolshed)

I am not sure if I wrote that the correct way corresponding to the run crate profile (this is a bit obscure to me)

@pauldg What do you think of this ?
Thanks !!

we go again !
I am launching this PR on a bit of work I did to ameliorate the RO-Crate plugin.

I updated the `_add_worfklow` function. I added the information on the author of the workflow:
- Name
- Orcid
The update checks if the workflow attribute 'creator_metadata' exist. If it exists, it writes into the ro-crate the name of the creator (if there is no name it leaves a "") same for the idenifier (by default i put the ORCID maybe it's not a good Idea and if there is no Orcid it leaves a ""). If there is no  'creator_metadata' nothing is written on the creator.

I created a function `_add_tools` that checks the different info in `workflow.steps` to add some tools details:
- Id of the tool
- tool's name (label if there is one or id if there is no label)
- tools' version
- tool's description: the update checks if the tool has an annotations attribute if it exist it is written as the description if it does not the description remains "")
- url to the the toolshed (not sure this part is relevant as I did not yet solve how to have the exact url to the tool in the toolshed and I just let the global url to the toolshed)

I am not sure if I wrote that the correct way corresponding to the run crate profile (this is a bit obscure to me)

@pauldg What do you think of this ?
Thanks !!

we go again !
I am launching this PR on a bit of work I did to ameliorate the RO-Crate plugin.

I updated the `_add_worfklow` function. I added the information on the author of the workflow:
- Name
- Orcid
The update checks if the workflow attribute 'creator_metadata' exist. If it exists, it writes into the ro-crate the name of the creator (if there is no name it leaves a "") same for the idenifier (by default i put the ORCID maybe it's not a good Idea and if there is no Orcid it leaves a ""). If there is no  'creator_metadata' nothing is written on the creator.

I created a function `_add_tools` that checks the different info in `workflow.steps` to add some tools details:
- Id of the tool
- tool's name (label if there is one or id if there is no label)
- tools' version
- tool's description: the update checks if the tool has an annotations attribute if it exist it is written as the description if it does not the description remains "")
- url to the the toolshed (not sure this part is relevant as I did not yet solve how to have the exact url to the tool in the toolshed and I just let the global url to the toolshed)

I am not sure if I wrote that the correct way corresponding to the run crate profile (this is a bit obscure to me)

@pauldg What do you think of this ?
Thanks !!

 A workflow, just like any other piece of software, can be formally correct and runnable but still lack a number of additional features that might help its reusability, interoperability, understandability, etc.
 
-One of the most useful additions to a workflow is a suite of tests, which help check that the workflow is operating as intended. A test case consists of a set of inputs and corresponding expected outputs, together with a procedure for comparing the workflow's actual outputs with the expected ones. It might be the case, in fact, that a test may be considered successful even if the actual outputs do not match the expected ones exactly, for instance because the computation involves a certain degree of randomness, or the output includes timestamps or randomly generated identifiers. Providing documentation is also important to help understand the workflow's purpose and mode of operation, its requirements, the effect of its parameters, etc. Even a single, well structured README file can go a long way towards getting users started with your workflow, especially if complemented by examples that include sample inputs and running instructions.
+One of the most useful additions to a workflow is a suite of tests, which help check that the workflow is operating as intended. A test case consists of a set of inputs and corresponding expected outputs, together with a procedure for comparing the workflow's actual outputs with the expected ones. It might be the case, in fact, that a test may be considered successful even if the actual outputs do not match the expected ones exactly, for instance because the computation involves a certain degree of randomness, or the output includes timestamps or randomly generated identifiers.
+
+Providing documentation is also important to help understand the workflow's purpose and mode of operation, its requirements, the effect of its parameters, etc. Even a single, well structured README file can go a long way towards getting users started with your workflow, especially if complemented by examples that include sample inputs and running instructions.

+> <tip-title>Using your own workflow</tip-title>

 ## Community best practices
 
-Though the practices listed above can be considered general enough to be applicable to any kind of software, individual communities usually add their own specific sets of rules and conventions that help users quickly find their way around software projects, understand them more easily and reuse them more effectively. The Galaxy community, for instance, has a [guide on best practices for maintaining workflows](https://planemo.readthedocs.io/en/latest/best_practices_workflows.html).

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the

| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| Rust            | `@conda-forge/help-rust`      |
| Go              | `@conda-forge/help-go`        |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io

All apologies in advance if your recipe PR does not receive prompt attention.
This is a high volume repository and the reviewers are volunteers. Review times
vary depending on the number of reviewers on a given language team and may be days
or weeks. We are always looking for more staged-recipe reviewers. If you are
interested in volunteering, please contact a member of @conda-forge/core.
We'd love to have the help!
-->

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| Rust            | `@conda-forge/help-rust`      |
| Go              | `@conda-forge/help-go`        |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io

All apologies in advance if your recipe PR does not receive prompt attention.
This is a high volume repository and the reviewers are volunteers. Review times
vary depending on the number of reviewers on a given language team and may be days
or weeks. We are always looking for more staged-recipe reviewers. If you are
interested in volunteering, please contact a member of @conda-forge/core.
We'd love to have the help!
-->

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [x] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [x] Package does not ship static libraries. If static libraries are needed, [follow CFEP-18](https://github.com/conda-forge/cfep/blob/main/cfep-18.md).
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [x] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).
- [ ] GitHub users listed in the maintainer section have posted a comment confirming they are willing to be listed there.
- [x] When in trouble, please check our [knowledge base documentation](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html) before pinging a team.

https://github.com/galaxyproject/iwc/blob/main/workflows/README.md#ensure-workflows-follow-best-practices :
> The "Identifier" field of a Creator added as a person should be filled with a fully qualified URI, e.g., https://orcid.org/0000-0002-1825-0097.

This PR adds a check which will emit a warning if a workflow creator is a person and their identifier is not a URI.

I'm aware this function is meant to mirror the Galaxy UI - I can try and make a matching PR for the UI, though I'm weaker on Vue/JS than Python. Thought I would open this PR first to confirm if that's worthwhile.

Context for the PR: my team is scraping Galaxy workflow RO-Crates from WorkflowHub and found a few where the generated author metadata uses just the ORCID number for the `@id` rather than the full ORCID URI. We are keen to help reduce those cases through improved linting!","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/planemo/issues/1458/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":1,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/planemo/issues/1458/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/planemo/issues/comments/2208688627","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/planemo/pull/1458#issuecomment-2208688627","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/planemo/issues/1458","id":2208688627,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAXoUaM6Dpenz","user":{"login":"elichad","id":20683271,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwNjgzMjcx","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20683271?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad","html_url":"https://github.com/elichad","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-07-04T10:57:13Z","updated_at":"2024-07-04T10:57:13Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Thanks @mvdbeek, fine with me! > The "Identifier" field of a Creator added as a person should be filled with a fully qualified URI, e.g., https://orcid.org/0000-0002-1825-0097.

This PR adds a check which will emit a warning if a workflow creator is a person and their identifier is not a URI.

I'm aware this function is meant to mirror the Galaxy UI - I can try and make a matching PR for the UI, though I'm weaker on Vue/JS than Python. Thought I would open this PR first to confirm if that's worthwhile.

Context for the PR: my team is scraping Galaxy workflow RO-Crates from WorkflowHub and found a few where the generated author metadata uses just the ORCID number for the `@id` rather than the full ORCID URI. We are keen to help reduce those cases  through improved linting Suggesting these changes after following the tutorial myself:
- updated output of `get_entities()` because `./ Dataset` always appears first
- added "name" property to Donald Duck because if you re-export the crate and then continue with the tutorial, the name can appear as `None` when you print authors in a later section causing confusion
- switched bash commands to Python where possible so it's easier to follow along with the auto-generated notebook
- added explanation of what happens when including the `exp/logs` dataset in the crate
- added extra CLI commands

One question:
The command-line interface section has the student clone an entire repository just to get one folder of test data to run `rocrate` on. I think it'd be better to host this test data in another repository by itself or as a downloadable zip file, which would simplify the commands needed - I'm happy to update the tutorial accordingly but what's the best approach (or most common in the GTN) to storing the data? Zenodo?

Requesting feedback from @simleo as a maintainer of the rocrate I've added user @patrick-austin as instructed in #3.

Merge this PR to add the user. Please do not rerender this PR or change it in any way. It has `[ci skip]` in the commit message to avoid pushing a new build and so the build configuration in the feedstock should not be changed.

Please contact [conda-forge/core](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/maintainer_faq.html#mfaq-contact-core) to have this PR merged, if the maintainer is unresponsive.

Hi! This is the friendly automated conda-forge-webservice.

I've added user @joelvdavies as instructed in #7.

Merge this PR to add the user. Please do not rerender this PR or change it in any way. It has `[ci skip]` in the commit message to avoid pushing a new build and so the build configuration in the feedstock should not be changed.

I've added user @joelvdavies as instructed in #7.

Merge this PR to add the user. Please do not rerender this PR or change it in any way. It has `[ci skip]` in the commit message to avoid pushing a new build and so the build configuration in the feedstock should not be changed.

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/14627","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/14627/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/14627/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/14627/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/14627","id":1374857139,"node_id":"I_kwDOAdw-Nc5R8qez","number":14627,"title":"Visible marker to distinguish required/optional tool params","user":{"login":"elichad","id":20683271,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwNjgzMjcx","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20683271?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad","html_url":"https://github.com/elichad","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2022-09-15T17:27:11Z","updated_at":"2022-09-15T17:27:11Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Describe the bug**
Tool params that are marked up with `optional=true` aren't distinguished from non-optional params in the UI. 

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Version: 22.01 (muongalaxy.stfc.ac.uk) and 22.05 (usegalaxy.eu)

**Expected behavior**
There should be a clear distinction between required and optional fields in the tool form UI (both visible and screen reader accessible).

There are a couple of common ways this is done elsewhere on the web:
- asterisk on required fields (usually red) 
- text '(optional)' after optional fields (often smaller and/or greyed-out)

The best option for Galaxy probably depends on whether the majority of fields across all tools are optional or required.

--

**Screenshots**
The second and fourth fields in this screenshot are optional, but there's no way to tell.
![Screenshot of tool form containing four fields with identical formatting](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/20683271/190468292-32ea283b-47d2-498f-922d-9367c3480a07.png)

Example from [this CI run](https://github.com/muon-spectroscopy-computational-project/muon-galaxy-tools/runs/7648604870?check_suite_focus=true):
```
Applying linter tool_urls... FAIL
.. ERROR: Error '503 Server Error: Service Temporarily Unavailable for url: https://pubs.acs.org/doi/10.1021/ct300849w' accessing https://doi.org/10.1021/ct300849w
.. ERROR: Error '503 Server Error: Service Temporarily Unavailable for url: https://pubs.acs.org/doi/10.1021/ct401002w' accessing https://doi.org/10.1021/ct401002w
.. ERROR: Error '503 Server Error: Service Temporarily Unavailable for url: https://pubs.acs.org/doi/10.1021/jp506557r' accessing https://doi.org/10.1021/jp506557r
.. ERROR: Error '503 Server Error: Service Temporarily Unavailable for url: https://pubs.acs.org/doi/10.1021/ct5009137' accessing https://doi.org/10.1021/ct5009137
.. ERROR: Error '503 Server Error: Service Temporarily Unavailable for url: https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/pssb.200301886' accessing https://doi.org/10.1002/pssb.200301886
.. INFO: URL OK https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0/legalcode
.. INFO: URL OK https://doi.org/10.1016/S0038-1098(99)00137-4

```
When I click through manually, the links work fine - both the full link and the DOI link. Not all the URLs raise errors, and the ones that fail do so consistently, so I don't think the service is 'temporarily unavailable' (this has been the same for months). Perhaps it's something specific that these websites are doing that blocks access, but then I'd expect a 403 or 401 like in #578 and #677.

Any thoughts on whether this is something that can be fixed on the Planemo side? If not I guess I'll just have to disable `--urls` in my CI checks.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/planemo/issues/1261/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/planemo/issues/1261/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

Didn't know what the "latest" release branch was for the purposes of adding datatypes, so have created another PR against 22.05 as well: #14485 

Added datatypes for a selection of CASTEP input and output files, which are used in the context of muon spectroscopy:
- `.castep`
- `.param`
- `.den_fmt`

See:
https://www.tcm.phy.cam.ac.uk/castep/documentation/WebHelp/content/modules/castep/expcastepfileformats.htm
https://www.tcm.phy.cam.ac.uk/castep/documentation/WebHelp/content/modules/castep/keywords/k_write_form_dens_castep.htm
https://github.com/muon-spectroscopy-computational-project/muon-galaxy/issues/15

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/pulls/comments/952420096","pull_request_review_id":1081787393,"id":952420096,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOAdw-Nc44xMcA","diff_hunk":"@@ -911,3 +912,115 @@ def _looks_like_yaml(self, file_prefix: FilePrefix):
             except yaml.YAMLError:
                 return False
             return False
+
+
+@build_sniff_from_prefix
+class Castep(Text):
+    """Report on the calculation (text)"""
+
+    file_ext = "castep"
+    sniff: typing.Callable
+
+    def sniff_prefix(self, file_prefix: FilePrefix):
+        """Determines whether the file is a CASTEP log"""
+        castep_header = [
+            "+-------------------------------------------------+",
+            "|                                                 |",
+            "|      CCC   AA    SSS  TTTTT  EEEEE  PPPP        |",
+            "|     C     A  A  S       T    E      P   P       |",
+            "|     C     AAAA   SS     T    EEE    PPPP        |",
+            "|     C     A  A     S    T    E      P           |",
+            "|      CCC  A  A  SSS     T    EEEEE  P           |",
+            "|                                                 |",
+            "+-------------------------------------------------+"
+        ]
+        handle = file_prefix.string_io()
+        for header_line in castep_header:
+            if handle.readline().strip() != header_line:
+                return False
+        return True
+
+
+@build_sniff_from_prefix
+class Param(Yaml):
+    """General input file (text)"""
+
+    file_ext = "param"
+    sniff: typing.Callable
+
+    def sniff_prefix(self, file_prefix: FilePrefix):
+        """
+        Modified version of the normal Yaml sniff that also checks the
+        dict keys for "general" CASTEP keywords, which are not case
+        sensitive
+        """
+        general_keywords = [
+            "BACKUP_INTERVAL",
+            "CALCULATE_STRESS",
+            "CALCULATE_DENSDIFF",
+            "CALCULATE_ELF",
+            "CALCULATE_HIRSHFELD",
+            "CHARGE_UNIT",
+            "CHECKPOINT",
+            "COMMENT",
+            "CONTINUATION",
+            "DATA_DISTRIBUTION",
+            "IPRINT",
+            "NUM_BACKUP_ITER",
+            "OPT_STRATEGY",
+            "PAGE_WVFNS",
+            "PRINT_CLOCK",
+            "PRINT_MEMORY_USAGE",
+            "RAND_SEED",
+            "REUSE",
+            "RUN_TIME",
+            "STOP",
+            "TASK",
+            "WRITE_CHECKPOINT",
+            "WRITE_FORMATTED_DENSITY",
+            "WRITE_FORMATTED_ELF",
+            "WRITE_FORMATTED_POTENTIAL",
+            "WRITE_ORBITALS"
+        ]
+        # Pattern used by SequenceSplitLocations
+        if file_prefix.file_size < 50000 and not file_prefix.truncated:
+            # If the file is small enough - don't guess just check.
+            try:
+                item = yaml.safe_load(file_prefix.contents_header)
+                assert isinstance(item, dict)
+            except yaml.YAMLError:
+                return False
+        else:
+            # If file is too big, load the first part. Trim the current line, in case it cut off in the middle of a key.
+            file_start = file_prefix.string_io().read(50000).strip().rsplit("\n", 1)[0]
+            try:
+                item = yaml.safe_load(file_start)
+                assert isinstance(item, dict)
+            except yaml.YAMLError:
+                return False
+
+        for key in item.keys():
+            assert isinstance(key, str)
+            if key.upper() in general_keywords:
+                return True
+        return False","path":"lib/galaxy/datatypes/text.py","position":103,"original_position":103,"commit_id":"bae4a9a74d0854e7dde653746b54548b42f06e3b","original_commit_id":"bae4a9a74d0854e7dde653746b54548b42f06e3b","user":{"login":"elichad","id":20683271,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwNjgzMjcx","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20683271?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad","html_url":"https://github.com/elichad","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"I can imagine us getting false positives from keys like "COMMENT" and "TASK". Didn't know what the "latest" release branch was for the purposes of adding datatypes, so have created another PR against 22.05 as well: #14486 

Added datatypes for a selection of CASTEP input and output files, which are used in the context of muon spectroscopy:
- `.castep`
- `.param`
- `.den_fmt`

See:
https://www.tcm.phy.cam.ac.uk/castep/documentation/WebHelp/content/modules/castep/expcastepfileformats.htm
https://www.tcm.phy.cam.ac.uk/castep/documentation/WebHelp/content/modules/castep/keywords/k_write_form_dens_castep.htm
https://github.com/muon-spectroscopy-computational-project/muon-galaxy/issues/15

Didn't know what the "latest" release branch was for the purposes of adding datatypes, so have created another PR against 22.05 as well: #14486 

Added datatypes for a selection of CASTEP input and output files, which are used in the context of muon spectroscopy:
- `.castep`
- `.param`
- `.den_fmt`

See:
https://www.tcm.phy.cam.ac.uk/castep/documentation/WebHelp/content/modules/castep/expcastepfileformats.htm
https://www.tcm.phy.cam.ac.uk/castep/documentation/WebHelp/content/modules/castep/keywords/k_write_form_dens_castep.htm
https://github.com/muon-spectroscopy-computational-project/muon-galaxy/issues/15

Didn't know what the "latest" release branch was for the purposes of adding datatypes, so have created another PR against 22.05 as well: #14486 

Added datatypes for a selection of CASTEP input and output files, which are used in the context of muon spectroscopy:
- `.castep`
- `.param`
- `.den_fmt`

https://github.com/muon-spectroscopy-computational-project/muon-galaxy/issues/15

Didn't know what the "latest" release branch was for the purposes of adding datatypes, so have created another PR against 22.05 as well: #14486 

Added datatypes for a selection of CASTEP input and output files, which are used in the context of muon spectroscopy:
- `.castep`
- `.param`
- `.den_fmt`

See:
https://www.tcm.phy.cam.ac.uk/castep/documentation/WebHelp/content/modules/castep/expcastepfileformats.htm
https://www.tcm.phy.cam.ac.uk/castep/documentation/WebHelp/content/modules/castep/keywords/k_write_form_dens_castep.htm
https://github.com/muon-spectroscopy-computational-project/muon-galaxy/issues/15

Didn't know what the "latest" release branch was for the purposes of adding datatypes, so have created another PR against 22.01 as well: #14485 

Added datatypes for a selection of CASTEP input and output files, which are used in the context of muon spectroscopy:
- `.castep`
- `.param`
- `.den_fmt`

See:
https://www.tcm.phy.cam.ac.uk/castep/documentation/WebHelp/content/modules/castep/expcastepfileformats.htm
https://www.tcm.phy.cam.ac.uk/castep/documentation/WebHelp/content/modules/castep/keywords/k_write_form_dens_castep.htm
https://github.com/muon-spectroscopy-computational-project/muon-galaxy/issues/15

Thank you very much for putting in this recipe PR!

This repository is very active, so if you need help with a PR, please let the
right people know. There are language-specific teams for reviewing recipes.

| Language        | Name of review team           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------- |
| python          | `@conda-forge/help-python`    |
| python/c hybrid | `@conda-forge/help-python-c`  |
| r               | `@conda-forge/help-r`         |
| java            | `@conda-forge/help-java`      |
| nodejs          | `@conda-forge/help-nodejs`    |
| c/c++           | `@conda-forge/help-c-cpp`     |
| perl            | `@conda-forge/help-perl`      |
| Julia           | `@conda-forge/help-julia`     |
| ruby            | `@conda-forge/help-ruby`      |
| other           | `@conda-forge/staged-recipes` |

Once the PR is ready for review, please mention one of the teams above in a
new comment. i.e. `@conda-forge/help-some-language, ready for review!`
Then, a bot will label the PR as 'review-requested'.

Due to GitHub limitations, first time contributors to conda-forge are unable
to ping conda-forge teams directly, but you can [ask a bot to ping the team][1]
using a special command in a comment on the PR to get the attention of the
`staged-recipes` team. You can also consider asking on our [Gitter channel][2]
if your recipe isn't reviewed promptly.

[1]: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/infrastructure.html#conda-forge-admin-please-ping-team
[2]: https://gitter.im/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io
-->

Checklist
- [x] Title of this PR is meaningful: e.g. "Adding my_nifty_package", not "updated meta.yaml".
- [x] License file is packaged (see [here](https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/blob/5eddbd7fc9d1502169089da06c3688d9759be978/recipes/example/meta.yaml#L64-L73) for an example).
- [x] Source is from official source.
- [x] Package does not vendor other packages. (If a package uses the source of another package, they should be separate packages or the licenses of all packages need to be packaged).
- [x] If static libraries are linked in, the license of the static library is packaged.
- [x] Build number is 0.
- [x] A tarball (`url`) rather than a repo (e.g. `git_url`) is used in your recipe (see [here](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#build-from-tarballs-not-repos) for more details).
- [x] GitHub users listed in the maintainer section have posted a comment confirming they are willing to be listed there.
- [x] When in trouble, please check our [knowledge base documentation](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html) before pinging a team.

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13936","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13936/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13936/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13936/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13936","id":1241981882,"node_id":"I_kwDOAdw-Nc5KByO6","number":13936,"title":"Keyboard navigation in new history: dataset list","user":{"login":"elichad","id":20683271,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwNjgzMjcx","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20683271?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad","html_url":"https://github.com/elichad","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2022-05-19T15:47:22Z","updated_at":"2022-05-19T15:47:22Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Describe the bug**

- [Elements should not have tabindex greater than zero](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.4/tabindex) - all the dataset items violate this principle and it makes the page confusing to navigate. The datasets should not be the first thing a keyboard user encounters on the page, and the individual dataset buttons ('View data' etc) are so far down the tab order relative to the data set list (the rest of the page is in between!) that I thought I just could not get to them with a keyboard.
- I expected that pressing Space on a dataset item would expand the dataset, but instead it activated the selection mode and selected the dataset. These should be two separate controls.
- Speaking of the selection mode, it's not clear how to deactivate it once it is activated. Pressing Space a second time deselected the checkbox, but the checkboxes themselves did not vanish. Pressing 'Select/deselect all' twice works, but tabbing to this is tedious at best and difficult at worst (especially with a funky tab order)

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Version: dev
Commit: 2f01b32b948a85e0ce5ef916aa7b66597ab94371

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Activate new history
2. Tab into dataset list
3. Press space
4. See checkbox activated
5. Press space again
6. See checkbox deactivated but still visible

**Expected behavior**
Pressing space expands/collapses the dataset","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13936/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13936/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13937","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13937/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13937/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13937/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13937","id":1241996906,"node_id":"I_kwDOAdw-Nc5KB15q","number":13937,"title":"Keyboard navigation in new history: history controls","user":{"login":"elichad","id":20683271,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwNjgzMjcx","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20683271?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad","html_url":"https://github.com/elichad","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2022-05-19T16:00:39Z","updated_at":"2022-05-19T16:00:39Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Describe the bug**

- When pressing the history edit button, a form appears to update the name and annotation, but the fields are before the edit button in the tab order, so you have to tab backwards to get to them - this is confusing.
- Similar issue where the filter field is not adjacent to the filter button in tab order
- Created tags have no focus indicator
- Created tags can't be deleted using keyboard
- Cannot tab to 'X' button in tag entry field
- Cannot tab to the panel collapse button or the resize control - these also don't seem to have accessible names or be labeled as buttons in any way

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Version: dev
Commit: 2f01b32b948a85e0ce5ef916aa7b66597ab94371

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Activate new history
2. Tab to history edit button & press
3. Tab backwards to find the form
4. Enter some tags
5. Tab to a created tag and try to delete it (enter, space, delete) - nothing happens
6. Try to tab to the 'X' in the tag field - cannot reach it

**Expected behavior**

- Upon pressing 'Edit,' the history name field appears next in the tab order.
- Created tags can be deleted using keyboard control
- Tag field can be cleared using keyboard control
- Can collapse & resize history panel using keyboard control","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13937/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13937/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13938","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13938/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13938/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13938/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13938","id":1242029055,"node_id":"I_kwDOAdw-Nc5KB9v_","number":13938,"title":"Accessibility of new history: buttons","user":{"login":"elichad","id":20683271,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwNjgzMjcx","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20683271?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad","html_url":"https://github.com/elichad","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2022-05-19T16:28:44Z","updated_at":"2022-05-19T16:28:44Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Describe the bug**

- It is completely unclear what the check mark buttons are for. Two of them have a hidden label 'Valid' and one is 'Ok' - what is pressing them supposed to do? Initially I thought the one next to the history edit fields was a 'confirm/save' type button. 
- Similarly, the 'revert history' button is similar to 'refresh' or 'undo' icons, and I have no idea what the button is supposed to do or what 'reverting' means in this case. 
- In both of these cases (and also in general, but to a lesser extent where icons are widely known), visual labels for the buttons would be helpful. As a voice control user I would have no idea how to click those two because I have to guess at labels based on icons.
- The dataset selection checkboxes have no accessible label.
- Repeated buttons like 'View data' should also indicate what dataset they are viewing (etc) in the accessible label - e.g. 'View data set 20'. You can get away without this slightly more if the buttons are properly nested within the parent list element/in the right tab order, but with the current tab order there's really no way to tell what buttons belong to what dataset if you just list the buttons (see screenshot and #13936 ).

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Version: dev
Commit: 2f01b32b948a85e0ce5ef916aa7b66597ab94371

**Expected behavior**
Buttons give a clear indication of what will happen when they are activated.

**Screenshots**
![History edit buttons](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/20683271/169345999-7cc5ea20-36c5-40ea-890b-8bf6b6e89295.png)
![Dataset buttons](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/20683271/169346148-98460814-2efc-4948-aeeb-f91465474d8e.png)
![NVDA list of buttons for new history](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/20683271/169350913-f2f86268-1e13-49c7-91ec-96c00680cde0.png)

the bug**

*Edit: oops, I thought I was testing on dev but I was actually on 22.01. Looks like a bunch of these have been fixed, yay*

- It is completely unclear what the check mark buttons are for. Two of them have a hidden label 'Valid' and one is 'Ok' - what is pressing them supposed to do? Initially I thought the one next to the history edit fields was a 'confirm/save' type button. 
- Similarly, the 'revert history' button is similar to 'refresh' or 'undo' icons, and I have no idea what the button is supposed to do or what 'reverting' means in this case. 
- In both of these cases (and also in general, but to a lesser extent where icons are widely known), visual labels for the buttons would be helpful. As a voice control user I would have no idea how to click those two because I have to guess at labels based on icons.
- The dataset selection checkboxes have no accessible label.
- Repeated buttons like 'View data' should also indicate what dataset they are viewing (etc) in the accessible label - e.g. 'View data set 20'. You can get away without this slightly more if the buttons are properly nested within the parent list element/in the right tab order, but with the current tab order there's really no way to tell what buttons belong to what dataset if you just list the buttons (see screenshot and #13936 ).

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Version: dev
Commit: 2f01b32b948a85e0ce5ef916aa7b66597ab94371

**Expected behavior**
Buttons give a clear indication of what will happen when they are activated.

**Screenshots**
![History edit buttons](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/20683271/169345999-7cc5ea20-36c5-40ea-890b-8bf6b6e89295.png)
![Dataset buttons](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/20683271/169346148-98460814-2efc-4948-aeeb-f91465474d8e.png)
![NVDA list of buttons for new history](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/20683271/169350913-f2f86268-1e13-49c7-91ec-96c00680cde0.png)

the bug**

*Edit: oops, I thought I was testing on dev but I was actually on 22.01. Looks like a bunch of these have been fixed, yay*

- Form inputs (e.g. history/dataset name) should have [visible](https://axe.deque.com/rule-help/en/label-not-present) and [persistent](https://axe.deque.com/rule-help/en/label-is-placeholder) labels - i.e. not just placeholders which vanish when you type. 
- The 'tags' field in particular is easy to mistake for a non-interactive element and would benefit from a text label and the tag image being persistent outside the field.
- The accessible name for a dataset list item is `<hid>:<name> button View data button Edit attributes button...` - I am not sure if this is what's meant to happen when you have buttons inside a list like this.
- The '?' link within the expanded dataset isn't an accessible name for screen readers (most don't read punctuation by default so the label is effectively empty)

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Version: dev
Commit: 2f01b32b948a85e0ce5ef916aa7b66597ab94371

**Screenshots**

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/pulls/comments/840452642","pull_request_review_id":928733587,"id":840452642,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOAdw-Nc4yGEoi","diff_hunk":"@@ -674,6 +795,689 @@ def set_peek(self, dataset):
             dataset.blurb = 'file purged from disk'
 
 
+@build_sniff_from_prefix
+class Cell(GenericMolFile):
+    """
+    CASTEP CELL format.
+    """
+
+    file_ext = "cell"
+    meta_error = False
+    MetadataElement(
+        name="atom_data",
+        default=[],
+        desc="Atom symbols and positions",
+        readonly=True,
+        visible=False,
+    )
+    MetadataElement(
+        name="number_of_atoms",
+        desc="Number of atoms",
+        readonly=True,
+        visible=True,
+    )
+    MetadataElement(
+        name="chemical_formula",
+        desc="Chemical formula",
+        readonly=True,
+        visible=True,
+    )
+    MetadataElement(
+        name="is_periodic",
+        desc="Periodic boundary conditions",
+        readonly=True,
+        visible=True,
+    )
+    MetadataElement(
+        name="lattice_parameters",
+        desc="Lattice parameters",
+        readonly=True,
+        visible=True,
+    )
+
+    def sniff_prefix(self, file_prefix: FilePrefix):
+        """
+        Try to guess if the file is a CASTEP CELL file.
+
+        A fingerprint for CELL files is the use of %BLOCK and %ENDBLOCK to
+        denote data blocks (not case sensitive).
+
+        >>> from galaxy.datatypes.sniff import get_test_fname
+        >>> fname = get_test_fname('Si_uppercase.cell')
+        >>> Cell().sniff(fname)
+        True
+        >>> fname = get_test_fname('Si_lowercase.cell')
+        >>> Cell().sniff(fname)
+        True
+        >>> fname = get_test_fname('Si.cif')
+        >>> Cell().sniff(fname)
+        False
+        """
+        start_found = False
+        for line in file_prefix.line_iterator():
+            if not start_found and line.lower().startswith('%block'):
+                start_found = True
+            elif start_found and line.lower().startswith('%endblock'):
+                return True
+        return False
+
+    def set_meta(self, dataset, **kwd):
+        """
+        Find Atom IDs for metadata.
+        """
+        self.meta_error = False
+        if ase_io:
+            # enhanced metadata
+            try:
+                ase_data = ase_io.read(dataset.file_name, format="castep-cell")
+            except Exception as e:
+                log.warning('%s, set_meta Exception during ASE read: %s', self, unicodify(e))
+                self.meta_error = True
+                return
+
+            try:
+                atom_data = [
+                    str(sym) + str(pos) for sym, pos in zip(ase_data.get_chemical_symbols(), ase_data.get_positions())
+                ]
+                chemical_formula = ase_data.get_chemical_formula()
+                pbc = ase_data.get_pbc()
+                lattice_parameters = ase_data.get_cell().cellpar()
+            except Exception as e:
+                log.warning('%s, set_meta Exception during ASE metadata collection: %s', self, unicodify(e))
+                self.meta_error = True
+                return
+
+            # CELL file can only have one molecule
+            dataset.metadata.number_of_molecules = 1
+            dataset.metadata.atom_data = atom_data
+            dataset.metadata.number_of_atoms = len(dataset.metadata.atom_data)
+            dataset.metadata.chemical_formula = chemical_formula
+            try:
+                dataset.metadata.is_periodic = 1 if pbc else 0
+            except ValueError:  # pbc is an array
+                dataset.metadata.is_periodic = 1 if pbc.any() else 0
+            dataset.metadata.lattice_parameters = list(lattice_parameters)
+
+        else:
+            # simple metadata
+            with open(dataset.file_name) as f:
+                cell = f.read()
+            try:
+                # atom position data follows this pattern:
+                # %BLOCK POSITIONS(_FRAC|_ABS)
+                # [position data, one line per atom]
+                # %ENDBLOCK POSITIONS(_FRAC|_ABS)
+                block = (
+                    re.search(
+                        r"\n%BLOCK POSITIONS([\s\S]*?)\n%ENDBLOCK POSITIONS",
+                        cell,
+                        flags=re.IGNORECASE,
+                    )
+                    .group(1)
+                    .split("\n")[1:]
+                )
+                atom_data = []
+                for atom in block:
+                    atom = atom.split()
+                    symbol = atom[0].lower().capitalize()
+                    position = [float(i) for i in atom[1:4]]
+                    atom_data.append(symbol + str(position))
+
+                # CELL file can only have one molecule
+                dataset.metadata.number_of_molecules = 1
+                dataset.metadata.atom_data = atom_data
+                dataset.metadata.number_of_atoms = len(dataset.metadata.atom_data)
+            except Exception as e:
+                log.warning('%s, set_meta Exception during simple metadata collection: %s', self, unicodify(e))","path":"lib/galaxy/datatypes/molecules.py","position":272,"original_position":272,"commit_id":"1bbaed2213b4628fbadef019187f2294ba713bfb","original_commit_id":"9ca846b7ab57462483756b61ea38f814fb33b573","user":{"login":"elichad","id":20683271,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwNjgzMjcx","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20683271?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad","html_url":"https://github.com/elichad","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"I think you're right that an all-or-nothing approach to metadata is more sensible. {"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/pulls/comments/846288678","pull_request_review_id":936692264,"id":846288678,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOAdw-Nc4ycVcm","diff_hunk":"@@ -674,6 +795,637 @@ def set_peek(self, dataset):
             dataset.blurb = 'file purged from disk'
 
 
+@build_sniff_from_prefix
+class Cell(GenericMolFile):
+    """
+    CASTEP CELL format.
+    """
+
+    file_ext = "cell"
+    meta_error = False
+    MetadataElement(
+        name="atom_data",
+        default=[],
+        desc="Atom symbols and positions",
+        readonly=True,
+        visible=False,
+    )
+    MetadataElement(
+        name="number_of_atoms",
+        desc="Number of atoms",
+        readonly=True,
+        visible=True,
+    )
+    MetadataElement(
+        name="chemical_formula",
+        desc="Chemical formula",
+        readonly=True,
+        visible=True,
+    )
+    MetadataElement(
+        name="is_periodic",
+        desc="Periodic boundary conditions",
+        readonly=True,
+        visible=True,
+    )
+    MetadataElement(
+        name="lattice_parameters",
+        desc="Lattice parameters",
+        readonly=True,
+        visible=True,
+    )
+
+    def sniff_prefix(self, file_prefix: FilePrefix):
+        """
+        Try to guess if the file is a CASTEP CELL file.
+
+        A fingerprint for CELL files is the use of %BLOCK and %ENDBLOCK to
+        denote data blocks (not case sensitive).
+
+        >>> from galaxy.datatypes.sniff import get_test_fname
+        >>> fname = get_test_fname('Si_uppercase.cell')
+        >>> Cell().sniff(fname)
+        True
+        >>> fname = get_test_fname('Si_lowercase.cell')
+        >>> Cell().sniff(fname)
+        True
+        >>> fname = get_test_fname('Si.cif')
+        >>> Cell().sniff(fname)
+        False
+        """
+        start_found = False
+        for line in file_prefix.line_iterator():
+            if not start_found and line.lower().startswith('%block'):
+                start_found = True
+            elif start_found and line.lower().startswith('%endblock'):
+                return True
+        return False
+
+    def set_meta(self, dataset, **kwd):
+        """
+        Find Atom IDs for metadata.
+        """
+        self.meta_error = False
+        if ase_io is None:
+            # Don't have optional dependency, can't set advanced values
+            return
+        else:
+            # enhanced metadata
+            try:
+                ase_data = ase_io.read(dataset.file_name, format="castep-cell")
+            except Exception as e:
+                log.warning('%s, set_meta Exception during ASE read: %s', self, unicodify(e))
+                self.meta_error = True
+                return
+
+            try:
+                atom_data = [
+                    str(sym) + str(pos) for sym, pos in zip(ase_data.get_chemical_symbols(), ase_data.get_positions())
+                ]
+                chemical_formula = ase_data.get_chemical_formula()
+                pbc = ase_data.get_pbc()
+                lattice_parameters = ase_data.get_cell().cellpar()
+            except Exception as e:
+                log.warning('%s, set_meta Exception during ASE metadata collection: %s', self, unicodify(e))
+                self.meta_error = True
+                return
+
+            # CELL file can only have one molecule
+            dataset.metadata.number_of_molecules = 1
+            dataset.metadata.atom_data = atom_data
+            dataset.metadata.number_of_atoms = len(dataset.metadata.atom_data)

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/pulls/comments/849619403","pull_request_review_id":941169006,"id":849619403,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOAdw-Nc4ypCnL","diff_hunk":"@@ -674,6 +795,637 @@ def set_peek(self, dataset):
             dataset.blurb = 'file purged from disk'
 
 
+@build_sniff_from_prefix
+class Cell(GenericMolFile):
+    """
+    CASTEP CELL format.
+    """
+
+    file_ext = "cell"
+    meta_error = False
+    MetadataElement(
+        name="atom_data",
+        default=[],
+        desc="Atom symbols and positions",
+        readonly=True,
+        visible=False,
+    )
+    MetadataElement(
+        name="number_of_atoms",
+        desc="Number of atoms",
+        readonly=True,
+        visible=True,
+    )
+    MetadataElement(
+        name="chemical_formula",
+        desc="Chemical formula",
+        readonly=True,
+        visible=True,
+    )
+    MetadataElement(
+        name="is_periodic",
+        desc="Periodic boundary conditions",
+        readonly=True,
+        visible=True,
+    )
+    MetadataElement(
+        name="lattice_parameters",
+        desc="Lattice parameters",
+        readonly=True,
+        visible=True,
+    )
+
+    def sniff_prefix(self, file_prefix: FilePrefix):
+        """
+        Try to guess if the file is a CASTEP CELL file.
+
+        A fingerprint for CELL files is the use of %BLOCK and %ENDBLOCK to
+        denote data blocks (not case sensitive).
+
+        >>> from galaxy.datatypes.sniff import get_test_fname
+        >>> fname = get_test_fname('Si_uppercase.cell')
+        >>> Cell().sniff(fname)
+        True
+        >>> fname = get_test_fname('Si_lowercase.cell')
+        >>> Cell().sniff(fname)
+        True
+        >>> fname = get_test_fname('Si.cif')
+        >>> Cell().sniff(fname)
+        False
+        """
+        start_found = False
+        for line in file_prefix.line_iterator():
+            if not start_found and line.lower().startswith('%block'):
+                start_found = True
+            elif start_found and line.lower().startswith('%endblock'):
+                return True
+        return False
+
+    def set_meta(self, dataset, **kwd):
+        """
+        Find Atom IDs for metadata.
+        """
+        self.meta_error = False
+        if ase_io is None:
+            # Don't have optional dependency, can't set advanced values
+            return
+        else:
+            # enhanced metadata
+            try:
+                ase_data = ase_io.read(dataset.file_name, format="castep-cell")
+            except Exception as e:
+                log.warning('%s, set_meta Exception during ASE read: %s', self, unicodify(e))
+                self.meta_error = True
+                return
+
+            try:
+                atom_data = [
+                    str(sym) + str(pos) for sym, pos in zip(ase_data.get_chemical_symbols(), ase_data.get_positions())
+                ]
+                chemical_formula = ase_data.get_chemical_formula()
+                pbc = ase_data.get_pbc()
+                lattice_parameters = ase_data.get_cell().cellpar()
+            except Exception as e:
+                log.warning('%s, set_meta Exception during ASE metadata collection: %s', self, unicodify(e))
+                self.meta_error = True
+                return
+
+            # CELL file can only have one molecule
+            dataset.metadata.number_of_molecules = 1
+            dataset.metadata.atom_data = atom_data
+            dataset.metadata.number_of_atoms = len(dataset.metadata.atom_data)
+            dataset.metadata.chemical_formula = chemical_formula
+            try:
+                dataset.metadata.is_periodic = 1 if pbc else 0","path":"lib/galaxy/datatypes/molecules.py","position":239,"original_position":240,"commit_id":"acb0e2a588bc732ed64992a7bf6d99ec7ac94abe","original_commit_id":"c5195fbd6101def40dc76a6cc9bae0ec7c32df17","user":{"login":"elichad","id":20683271,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwNjgzMjcx","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20683271?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad","html_url":"https://github.com/elichad","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Hmm, interesting. {"action":"closed","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/12371","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/12371/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/12371/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/12371/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/12371","id":978102097,"node_id":"MDU6SXNzdWU5NzgxMDIwOTc=","number":12371,"title":"New molecule/atomic structure datatypes","user":{"login":"elichad","id":20683271,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwNjgzMjcx","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20683271?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad","html_url":"https://github.com/elichad","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2021-08-24T13:29:16Z","updated_at":"2022-04-20T14:26:34Z","closed_at":"2022-04-20T14:26:34Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"We (the muon folks) need to support a few new datatypes. These are all different ways of encoding atomic structures. The types are:

- .cell ([CASTEP CELL file](https://www.tcm.phy.cam.ac.uk/castep/documentation/WebHelp/content/modules/castep/expcastepfilecell.htm))
- .cif ([Crystallographic Information Framework](https://www.iucr.org/resources/cif))
- .xyz ([XYZ format](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/XYZ_file_format))

These seem like they would fit into the 'molecules' category of datatypes (certainly there is some scope overlap with PDB files which are already supported). Though I see #12364 and now I'm unclear if we can (or should) actually add these datatypes as part of our tool suite. There may well be more datatypes needed in the future.

These file types can be hard to sniff due to flexibility in their content and the existence of other similar file types, so we propose using existing functions in the open-source [Atomic Simulation Environment](https://gitlab.com/ase/ase) to do the sniffing, by trying to read the whole file as the specified data type (this also helps with setting metadata etc.).

I've done some [preliminary work](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/compare/dev...muon-spectroscopy-computational-project:eli/add-data-types) on this which I can make into a PR if this all sounds appropriate.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/12371/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/12371/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13620","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13620/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13620/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13620/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13620","id":1180508192,"node_id":"I_kwDOAdw-Nc5GXSAg","number":13620,"title":"Visualizations are not built correctly when using custom visualization directory","user":{"login":"elichad","id":20683271,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwNjgzMjcx","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20683271?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad","html_url":"https://github.com/elichad","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/elichad/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2022-03-25T08:48:11Z","updated_at":"2022-03-25T08:48:11Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Describe the bug**
When using custom `visualization_plugins_directory`, the plugins in that folder are not built into `/static/plugins/<visualization_plugins_directory>` during the client build. In the UI, the visualizer names are displayed under the Visualize tab, but trying to run them gives a 404 error for chart types, and pulls the plugin from `/static/plugins/visualizations` for the mako type (i.e. the wrong location)

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Version: 22.01 

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Create a new folder (e.g. `config/plugins/test_visualizations`) and populate it with `common/ example/ editor/ visualizations.dtd additional_template_paths.xml` from `config/plugins/visualizations` (symlink or copy)         
2. In galaxy.yml set `visualization_plugins_directory` to the directory you created
3. Build the client/start galaxy (I used ./run.sh)
4. Upload some tabular data to galaxy
5. Go to the Visualize tab 
6. Try to launch **example** visualizer – see 404 error in console
7. Try to launch **editor** visualizer – should work, but checking the response to the `http://localhost:8080/plugins/visualizations/editor/show?dataset_id=59606d2a36c77a56` request shows that it is using the wrong script path.

**Expected behavior**
All visualizers from `visualization_plugins_directory` are built into `/static/plugins` **and** all visualizer templates point to that location at runtime.

**Additional context**
Manually adding the new directory to the paths in client/gulpfile.js causes the plugins to be built into the correct location, but mako templates still point to the wrong place. #13168/#13176 added a text preview for the YAML filetype, but it doesn't work in Firefox. Firefox sees the intended mediatype, but still wants you to download it to view. Chrome allows you to preview the file in the Galaxy UI as intended.

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Observed both on usegalaxy.eu `{"version_major": "21.09", "version_minor": "1.dev0"}`
and on the current tip of dev 6df8d1ae5b54bbb0961576622066a3d2b95f6728 . 
Behaviour is the same on both Mac and Windows.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Use Firefox
2. Upload a file with the yaml datatype
3. Click "View data" in the history
4. See download dialog

**Expected behavior**
File is previewed like other text files.

**Screenshots**
![Firefox download dialog for a file of type text/x-yaml](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/20683271/152193192-7a5ba6af-c4e2-4b30-b3ac-973b86712570.png)

**Additional context**
We've only tested Firefox and Chrome/Chromium. I'm not sure if other browsers behave as intended or not.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13295/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13295/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null}}
